description_long: This is a medium-sized residential house with a red, gabled roof featuring overlapping shingles and a prominent brick chimney. The exterior walls are clad in light-colored horizontal siding, and the base of the house is finished with a brick trim. The front facade includes a small covered porch with a peaked roof, wooden steps, and a simple entryway. Large rectangular windows are symmetrically placed on the front and side, while the rear and one side have minimal or no window openings. The overall design is practical and unadorned, suitable for suburban or small-town neighborhoods.
description: A two-story house with a red gabled shingle roof, light horizontal siding, brick trim, and a small covered front porch with steps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a peaked porch, two large windows, and the prominent gabled roof.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the full expanse of the red shingle roof, chimney, and plain siding with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the sloped roof, plain siding, and absence of windows or doors on this side.
description_view_3: Side view with a clear look at the roof overhang and the side wall, as well as a partial view of the front porch.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple covered porch with peaked roof, wooden steps, and a single main door.
